text.name: ui_add_entry_title
text.text: Please enter a name for your crop rotation.

text.name: ui_remove_crop_rotation
text.text: Are you sure you want to delete the crop rotation '%s'?

text.name: helpLine_planner_description
text.text: The crop rotation planner can be accessed in the in-game menu and allows you to plan different crop rotations in advance. To do this, you can create new crop rotations with your own name. Once a crop rotation has been created, you can add or remove additional crops (at least one crop is required, which cannot be deleted). To do this, simply press the +/- button. The available crops can be selected using the sliders. It is also possible to select a catch crop, which is planted before the actual crop harvest. The expected yield is then displayed directly below the sliders. The yield is calculated from the last two previous crops. The previous crops for the fruit in the first and second positions are filled in at the end of the list.

text.name: helpLine_planner_example_title
text.text: Crop rotation with fewer than 3 fruits

text.name: helpLine_planner_example_description
text.text: The last two previous crops are required to calculate the yield. If these are not available in the planner, the crop rotation starts from the beginning. If, for example, a crop rotation has only one crop, the same crop is entered for both previous crops.

text.name: helpLine_planner_scrolling_description
text.text: Since the crop rotation planner consists of two nested lists, the mouse position is extremely important when scrolling. When the mouse is over the area for the crop rotation titles, all crop rotations are scrolled through. When the mouse is over the area for the crops, the crops of the respective crop rotation are scrolled through.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_description
text.text: The crops that were previously grown on the field have an impact on the yield of the current crop. This can have positive or negative effects. The following factors have an influence:

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_monoculture
text.text: Monocultures have a negative impact on yield. If the last two crops are the same as the current one, there will be a 5% loss in yield.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_breakPeriods
text.text: Certain crops cannot be grown every year, but need a certain number of breaks before they can be grown again on the same field without negative effects. These breaks can vary from plant to plant. If you do not observe these breaks, you will receive a 10% loss for each break not observed.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_foreCrops
text.text: Predecessor crops can have a negative, positive, or neutral effect on the current yield. Each plant has predecessor crops that are good, very good, or bad as predecessor crops. If you adhere to this, you can expect a higher yield. However, if you do not pay attention to this, you must expect losses. For each good preceding crop, there is a 10% or 5% yield bonus (depending on whether it was planted as the last or second-to-last preceding crop). For each very good preceding crop, there is a 15% or 10% yield bonus. For each poor preceding crop, there is a 25% or 20% yield loss.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_fallow
text.text: If you leave a field fallow (for at least 12 months), you will receive a 5% bonus on the next harvest, as the soil can recover during this time.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_catchCrop
text.text: Certain catch crops have a good, very good, or bad influence on the next harvest. These vary depending on which catch crop was grown. Good cover crops give a 10% yield bonus. Very good cover crops give a 15% bonus. Bad cover crops result in a 10% yield loss.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_wheat_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: maize, soybeans, potatoes, sugar beets, beetroot, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_wheat_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: wheat, barley, grass

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_barley_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: maize, soybeans, potatoes, sugar beets, beetroot

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_barley_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, oats, beans, peas, canola, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_oat_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: wheat, barley

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_oat_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: maize, soybeans, peas, potatoes, canola, sugar beets, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_sorghum_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: beans, peas, soybeans, wheat, barley, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_soybean_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: wheat, maize, sugar beets

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_soybean_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: Barley, oats, potatoes

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_soybean_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: Soybeans, peas, sunflowers, beans, canola

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_greenbean_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: wheat, canola, sunflowers, potatoes, sugar beets

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_greenbean_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: barley, oats, maize

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_greenbean_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: soybeans, beans, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_pea_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: wheat, barley, maize, potatoes

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_pea_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: canola, sunflowers, sugar beets

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_pea_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: soybeans, beans, peas, oats

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_maize_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, potatoes

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_canola_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: barley, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_canola_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, oats, grass

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_canola_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: maize, potatoes, canola, sugar beets, soybeans, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_sunflower_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, grass

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_sunflower_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: sunflowers, canola, soybeans, peas, beans

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_potato_very_good
text.text: Very good preceding crops: soybeans, beans, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_potato_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, grass

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_potato_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: potatoes, sugar beets, canola, sunflowers

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_sugarbeet_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, peas, grass

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_sugarbeet_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: maize, soybeans, canola, sugar beets

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_onion_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, soybeans, beans, peas, potatoes

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_onion_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: onions, carrots, parsnips, sugar beets, beetroot, spinach, sunflowers, canola

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_beetroot_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, soybeans, beans, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_beetroot_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: potatoes, sugar beets, spinach

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_carrot_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, soybeans, beans, peas, potatoes

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_parsnip_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, soybeans, beans, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_spinach_good
text.text: Good preceding crops: wheat, barley, oats, soybeans, beans, peas

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_spinach_bad
text.text: Bad preceding crops: Spinach, potatoes, sugar beets

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_catchCrops_description
text.text: Catch crops only affect the crop that is sown immediately afterwards. They can have a positive or negative effect. Here, too, there is a distinction between very good, good, and bad crops. In the best case, the yield can be increased by 15%. In the good case, only by 10%. In the worst case, a catch crop can even cause a 10% loss in yield.

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_oilseedradish_very_good
text.text: Very good influence: maize, potatoes, sunflowers, oats

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_oilseedradish_good
text.text: Good influence: wheat, barley, sorghum, sugar beets, onions, beetroot, carrots, parsnips, spinach

text.name: helpLine_crop_rotations_values_oilseedradish_bad
text.text: Bad influence: canola, soybeans, peas, beans
